methodology_sections: 1.1 Asset Inventory - Financial assets, state assets, privilege assets, reputation assets
1.3 System Architecture Mapping - Contract hierarchies, dependencies, upgrade patterns, access control
2.1 Entry Point Enumeration - Public/external functions, callbacks, parameter analysis

financial_assets: ETH held by RollupCreator (temporary; used to fund L2 factory deployment retryables; refundable remainder to caller)
ERC20 fee token transfers into Inbox during factory deployment (RollupCreator._deployFactories)
ERC20 stakeToken balances held by RollupCore/Rollup (validator deposits; withdrawable funds accounting)
ERC20 stakeToken balances held by EdgeChallengeManager (first stake per rival set kept for refund; excess stakes forwarded to excessStakeReceiver)
ERC20 stakeToken balances held by AbsBoldStakingPool / AssertionStakingPool / EdgeStakingPool (user pooled deposits)
Refund flows: EdgeChallengeManager.refundStake refunds stakeToken to edge.staker
Refund flows: RollupCore withdrawable funds (internal ledger) ultimately paid out via RollupUser.withdrawStakerFunds (not in provided code but referenced)
Gas refund mechanism hooks in SequencerInbox (GasRefundEnabled / IGasRefunder) affecting who receives L2 reimbursement credit
DAS keyset validity registry in SequencerInbox gating acceptance of DAS batches (indirectly impacts DA availability/fees)
state_assets: SequencerInbox.bridge (IBridge pointer; controls enqueue and delayed accumulator reads)
SequencerInbox.rollup (IOwnable pointer; used for owner checks; can be updated)
SequencerInbox.batchPosterManager (delegated authority over batch poster / sequencer allowlists)
SequencerInbox.delayBlocks/futureBlocks/delaySeconds/futureSeconds (time bounds for batch headers and force inclusion rules)
SequencerInbox.buffer (DelayBufferTypes.BufferData: bufferBlocks, max, threshold, prevBlockNumber, replenishRateInBasis, prevSequencedBlockNumber)
DelayBuffer library logic determining buffer depletion/replenishment and proof requirement state (buffer.isSynced/isUpdatable)

RollupCore config variables: chainId, confirmPeriodBlocks, baseStake, wasmModuleRoot, challengeManager, challengeGracePeriodBlocks, inbox, bridge, outbox, rollupEventInbox, loserStakeEscrow, stakeToken, minimumAssertionPeriod, validatorWhitelistDisabled, anyTrustFastConfirmer, isValidator[address]
EdgeChallengeManager edge store (EdgeStore internal store: edges, rival mappings, timer caches, confirmedRivals, firstRivals, hasMadeLayerZeroRival, etc. via library)
EdgeChallengeManager configuration: excessStakeReceiver, stakeToken, stakeAmounts[], challengePeriodBlocks, assertionChain, oneStepProofEntry, LAYERZERO_* heights, NUM_BIGSTEP_LEVEL

Creators: BridgeCreator.ethBasedTemplates/erc20BasedTemplates; RollupCreator template pointers and validatorWalletCreator; BOLDUpgradeAction immutable deployment settings; StateHashPreImageLookup.preImages mapping
privilege_assets: Rollup ownership / admin (RollupProxy admin; RollupAdminLogic functions reachable only via proxy admin path)
SequencerInbox onlyRollupOwner authority (setMaxTimeVariation, setValidKeyset, invalidateKeysetHash, setBatchPosterManager, setBufferConfig)
SequencerInbox batchPosterManager authority (shared with rollup owner): setIsBatchPoster, setIsSequencer
Batch poster privilege (SequencerInbox.isBatchPoster): ability to append L2 batches via multiple methods (calldata/blob) and influence ordering
BridgeCreator owner (can update templates for bridge/sequencerInbox/inbox/outbox/rei)
RollupCreator owner (can setTemplates to change deployed rollup components)
ProxyAdmin ownership (controls upgrades for TransparentUpgradeableProxy instances for bridge stack; transferred to UpgradeExecutor during rollup creation)
UUPS-style upgrade authority in RollupAdminLogic via DoubleLogicUUPSUpgradeable (primary and secondary implementations)
BOLDUpgradeAction indirectly exercises upgrade power through provided ProxyAdmins and upgrade calls (intended to be executed by timelock/executor governance)

EdgeChallengeManager validator whitelist enforcement depends on assertionChain.validatorWhitelistDisabled() and assertionChain.isValidator(msg.sender) (privilege gating edge creation when whitelist enabled)
reputation_assets: Validator status/whitelist (RollupCore.isValidator; EdgeChallengeManager createLayerZeroEdge enforces when whitelist enabled)
Assertion confirmation state (_latestConfirmed, assertion statuses) impacting protocol correctness/finality reputation
DAS keyset registry validity (dasKeySetInfo) impacting data availability trust assumptions
Sequencer/batch poster authorization sets (isSequencer/isBatchPoster) impacting liveness/censorship perceptions
Staking pool depositor balances and successful refunds (AbsBoldStakingPool.depositBalance; pool/rollup interaction correctness)
Challenge outcome integrity (EdgeChallengeManager confirmed edges and timing cache correctness) impacting dispute resolution reputation

system_architecture.inheritance_hierarchy: SequencerInbox inherits DelegateCallAware and GasRefundEnabled and implements ISequencerInbox. RollupAdminLogic inherits RollupCore, implements IRollupAdmin, and inherits DoubleLogicUUPSUpgradeable (UUPS-like primary+secondary upgrade). RollupCore inherits PausableUpgradeable. RollupCreator and BridgeCreator inherit OZ Ownable. RollupProxy inherits AdminFallbackProxy (custom admin/secondary-impl proxy). AbsBoldStakingPool is abstract and implemented by AssertionStakingPool and EdgeStakingPool. EdgeChallengeManager inherits Initializable and implements IEdgeChallengeManager (with constructor disabling initializers).
external_dependencies: OpenZeppelin: Ownable, Initializable, PausableUpgradeable, ProxyAdmin, TransparentUpgradeableProxy, UpgradeableBeacon (imported), Create2, Address, SafeERC20
OffchainLabs: DelegateCallAware, GasRefundEnabled, IGasRefunder, ArbitrumChecker, Messages, various bridge/rollup interfaces
EIP-4844 reader: IReader4844 (blob data hashes and blob base fee)
system_architecture.upgrade_patterns: Uses multiple upgrade patterns: (1) TransparentUpgradeableProxy for bridge stack components (Bridge, SequencerInbox, Inbox, Outbox, RollupEventInbox, ChallengeManager in creator/upgrade flows) governed by ProxyAdmin; (2) Custom RollupProxy (AdminFallbackProxy) with two logic implementations (admin/user) and initialization via initializeProxy; RollupAdminLogic uses DoubleLogicUUPSUpgradeable enabling upgrades of primary and secondary logic (UUPS-like) under proxy-admin routing; (3) BOLDUpgradeAction performs coordinated upgrades across proxies and migrates to a new RollupProxy via CREATE2.
system_architecture.access_control_structure: SequencerInbox uses rollup.owner() as owner authority plus batchPosterManager shared authority. RollupAdminLogic comments indicate functions are only reachable by proxy admin (upgrade executor) so lacks explicit onlyOwner modifiers; pause/resume gated by PausableUpgradeable's internal state but externally callable via admin path. RollupCreator/BridgeCreator gated by Ownable. EdgeChallengeManager gates createLayerZeroEdge by Rollup validator whitelist when enabled, otherwise permissionless. Staking pools are permissionless for deposit/withdraw; pool 'protocol actions' (createAssertion/createEdge, makeStakeWithdrawable, withdrawStakeBackIntoPool) are permissionless as written.
access_control_notes_defects: RollupAdminLogic: public/external admin functions (pause/resume/setOwner/etc.) rely on being reachable only through proxy-admin routing; if proxy wiring/admin separation is misconfigured, these become fully permissionless and catastrophic.
StateHashPreImageLookup.set() is permissionless and can be front-run; safety relies on BOLDUpgradeAction verifying hash correctness and timelock/executor sequencing.

state_changes: buffer (indirectly via delayProof requirement gating; no update here unless delay bufferable proof path used elsewhere)
deploys new TransparentUpgradeableProxy instances via BridgeCreator
Pauses old rollup and force refunds some stakers (via old admin logic)
Upgrades old rollup secondary implementation to patched user logic
Sets validators and whitelist disabled flag on new rollup
Transfers new rollup ownership to actual owner
deploys 5 TransparentUpgradeableProxy instances (bridge, sequencerInbox, inbox, rollupEventInbox, outbox)

external_calls: IRollupAdmin.initialize(config, connectedContracts) via delegatecall through proxy init

state_changes: ERC20 allowance from pool to rollup (increase)
ERC20 allowance from pool to challengeManager (increase)
deploys new AssertionStakingPool via CREATE2 with salt=0
deploys new EdgeStakingPool via CREATE2 with salt=0
ERC20 transfers of stakeToken (to self or excessStakeReceiver)
store.edges[...] (creates upper child, may create lower child)
store child links/timer cache fields (via library)
store.confirmedRivals[mutualId] and edge confirmed state (via library)

upgradeability_surface.notes: Designed to be used behind a proxy (onlyDelegated). Uses immutables for reader4844/maxDataSize/deployTimeChainId/hostChainIsArbitrum/isUsingFeeToken/isDelayBufferable; upgrades must preserve constructor-immutables expectations (NativeTokenMismatch check in initialize).

initializer_guards: initializeProxy checks admin/implementation/secondaryImplementation slots are zero before _initialize
upgradeability_surface.notes: Custom AdminFallbackProxy with dual implementations. Misconfiguration of admin routing can expose RollupAdminLogic functions permissionlessly. Storage collision risk exists between primary/secondary logic implementations if not carefully managed.

Uses DoubleLogicUUPSUpgradeable allowing upgrades of primary and secondary logic. _authorizeUpgrade and _authorizeSecondaryUpgrade are empty (no checks) because access is assumed enforced by proxy admin separation; if that assumption fails, upgrades are catastrophic.
Factory deploying TransparentUpgradeableProxy instances; not itself upgradeable.
Factory deploying RollupProxy and multiple TransparentUpgradeableProxy instances; not itself upgradeable.
Typically deployed behind TransparentUpgradeableProxy (as in RollupCreator/BOLDUpgradeAction). Initialization sets stake token/amounts and edge heights; upgrades must preserve EdgeStore layout.
Not upgradeable; orchestrates upgrades via external ProxyAdmin-controlled proxies.
Non-upgradeable staking pools deployed via CREATE2(salt=0) by creators; address determinism depends on creator address and bytecode.
